VOL LANDSCAPE & ANALYTICS

The VIX at 27.5 sits in backwardation with VIX3M trading 190bp below, marking the steepest inversion in six weeks as dealers prepare for vol reset. Broad equity IV ranks have spiked to 90th percentile across SPY, EEM, and IWM while put skew reaches extreme 99th percentile levels. Credit spreads remain contained despite equity vol expansion, creating a cross-asset divergence that typically resolves within 48 hours.

VRP: Front-month IV trading 4-6 points above realized vol across major indices — aggressive vol selling premium available for patient capital
SKEW: Put skew at 99th percentile in SPY/QQQ signals maximum defensive positioning and creates asymmetric call upside reward
TERM STRUCTURE: Steep backwardation in high-beta names (AMD, META, ARM) with front-week premiums 15-20% rich to fair value

Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 09:33 ET
Index put hedging surge contradicts single-name call buying amid dealer gamma amplification
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Extreme P/C divergence signals institutional dispersion hedge: index puts (1.55) against single-name calls (0.78) ahead of Core PCE Friday.
  • 2. Negative $14.8B SPY gamma creates amplified volatility environment where modest moves cascade into larger directional pressure.
  • 3. NVDA's massive 91k call block contrasts with equally large put protection, suggesting event positioning around AI earnings cycle.
NARRATIVE — dispersion hedge pre-PCE

The most striking signal today is the extreme divergence between index put protection and single-name call positioning, suggesting sophisticated dispersion hedging ahead of Friday's Core PCE print. Index P/C at 1.55 reflects heavy institutional put buying in SPY and QQQ, while single-name P/C at 0.78 shows concentrated call demand — a classic setup where smart money hedges macro downside while maintaining individual equity upside exposure. This bifurcation intensifies under the current negative $14.8B SPY gamma regime, where dealer hedging amplifies any directional moves beyond the $665 gamma flip level. NVDA's options flow exemplifies this tension, with the largest call block of 91k Mar 27 180C matched by substantial 69k put volume at the 177.5 strike. The concentration in short-dated strikes suggests positioning around imminent AI infrastructure earnings from hyperscalers, where NVDA historically gaps on guidance revisions. Millennium's 93% Q4 position increase and options overlay suggests institutional conviction despite technical death cross signals. Energy emerges as the cross-asset outlier, with XLE's 85k call block aligning with golden cross technicals and 78 RSI — overbought but momentum-driven. This contrasts sharply with the Vol High/Skew Flat divergence in sector ETFs like XLC and XLU, where elevated IV isn't matched by put skew, suggesting vol sellers are fading fear premiums into the PCE event.

Layer 1: Sentiment -32Layer 2: GEX -$14.8BLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.55/0.78Layer 9: Core PCE 1d

DESKUS Today's Top News11:57 AM ET
Synopsis (last modified 10:51 ET on 25-Mar-26) US equities higher: Dow +0.80%, S&P 500 +0.86%, Nasdaq +1.34%, Russell 2000 +1.46% * US equities higher in Wednesday morning trading. Comes after stocks put in mixed to weaker performance Tuesday, though major averages all pacing for solid weekly gains. Big tech higher, while semis, software also firmer. Other outperformers include China tech, airlines, industrial metals, banks, credit cards, PE, payment rails, and trucking. Laggards include memory
Synopsis (last modified 10:51 ET on 25-Mar-26) US equities higher: Dow +0.80%, S&P 500 +0.86%, Nasdaq +1.34%, Russell 2000 +1.46% * US equities higher in Wednesday morning trading. Comes after stocks put in mixed to weaker performance Tuesday, though major averages all pacing for solid weekly gains. Big tech higher, while semis, software also firmer. Other outperformers include China tech, airlines, industrial metals, banks, credit cards, PE, payment rails, and trucking. Laggards include memory (GOOGL TurboQuant update), energy, ag chemicals, homebuilders, exchanges, casual diners, athletic apparel, staples, and MCOs. Treasuries firmer though off best levels; with yields down 5-7 bp after a big backup on Tuesday. Dollar index flat. Gold up 3.6%. Silver up 5.1%. Bitcoin futures up 2.9%. WTI crude down 4%. Brent down 4%. * Oil down, yields down, stocks up. Risk on following report that hit just after the close yesterday of a US proposal for a one-month ceasefire. Also some focus on how Vance will be involved in the negotiations and additional reports mentioned the potential for high-level peace talks with Iran as soon as Thursday. However, Iran rejected ceasefire, called US talks illogical, and that truce and talks not viable in current conditions. Added to earlier skepticism given ceasefire demands suggest the two sides are very far apart, Iran's military rejected the idea of negotiations, Iran continues to control the Strait of Hormuz and more US troops are headed to the region. Big debate right now is whether the conflict is finally on a credible de-escalation path or if the recent stabilization is more reflective of the extent of the concerns surrounding longer duration scenarios that ramped over the course of the weekend. * February import prices up 1.3% m/m, highest since Mar-22, while export prices up 1.5% m/m, highest since May-22. Treasury selling $70B of 5-year notes following a very weak 2-year note auction on Tuesday that tailed the most in three years. Fed Governor Miran speaks after the market close. Looking ahead, initial claims the lone release for Thursday morning. Fed's Cook, Miran, Jefferson and Barr all scheduled to speak after the close on Thursday. Final University of Michigan consumer sentiment (and inflation expectations) for March caps off the week on Friday. Fed's Daly and Paulson also scheduled to speak on Friday morning. * Jury said META must pay $375M for endangering children online. Company also in the headlines with report it is targeting a $9T valuation with a new executive incentive program. In addition, the co-founders of Manus, the startup it recently acquired, have reportedly been restricted from leaving China. SpaceX reportedly could file for a $75B+ IPO within days. OpenAI confirmed it is discontinuing its Sora video platform app. ARM a big gainer on entry into CPU market and long-term guidance. Memory names (including MU, SNDK) lower after GOOGL TurboQuant compression algorithm announcement. HOOD announced $1.5B buyback program. DOW to hike plastics prices by more than previously signaled. ONON CEO stepping down and company's co-founders will become co-CEOs. TERN to be acquired by MRK for approx. $6B. VCTR withdrew bid for JHG. KBH missed on fiscal Q1, lowered FY guidance and noted conflict in Middle East has introduced an additional layer of uncertainty for consumers. AIR beat and raised FY organic sales growth outlook on strength across primary end markets. CHWY Q4 EBITDA beat while net active customer additions also ahead. WOR Q3 results ahead. DAN higher following 2030 targets (and buybacks) into analyst day. Notable Gainers: * +22.9% SRPT (Sarepta Therapeutics): Announced positive early data for its Phase 1/2 trial of SRP-1001 for facioscapulohumeral muscular dystrophy type 1 and SRP-1003 for myotonic dystrophy type 1. * +20.2% BRZE (Braze): Q4 revenue and margins beat; billings, customer count topped consensus; Q1 and FY revenue guidance ahead of the Street; authorized a $100M buyback; analysts positive on organic growth acceleration amid AI tailwinds. * +14.8% ARM (Arm Holdings): Company entering into CPU market, selling its own chips for first time, forecasting $15B in annual chip sales within five years; announced Meta as first major customer on Tuesday; also announced longer-term forecasts, including revenue of ~$25B within five years, around five-times current levels. * +13.5% CHWY (Chewy, Inc.): Q4 EBITDA and EBITDA margins beat, revenue essentially in line; active customers also in line, up 4% y/y, while net active customer additions beat expectations; Q1 and FY26 net sales outlook both stronger than Street consensus. * +10.0% SATS (EchoStar): Helped by news SpaceX expected to submit its IPO application as early as this week; company has an ownership stake after selling some spectrum to SpaceX last fall. * +9.5% AIR (AAR Corp): FQ3 earnings, revenue, and EBITDA margin beat; organic growth well ahead of consensus with notable strength in Parts Supply; raised FY revenue- and organic-growth guidance. * +6.2% HOOD (Robinhood Markets): Announced $1.5B share repurchase program. * +5.2% TERN (Terns Pharmaceuticals): Agreed to be acquired by Merck (MRK) for $53 a share in cash in a deal valued at $6.7B; represents a 6% premium to prior close; deal expected to close in Q2. * +2.7% PLTR (Palantir Technologies): Reportedly working with Anduril to develop software for Trump's Golden Dome antimissile shield. * +2.1% GM (General Motors): Upgraded to outperform from peer perform at Wolfe Research; cited full-size pickup refresh, further reductions in warranty costs, a lower net tariff burden, and improved electric vehicle losses. * +1.3% HON (Honeywell International): Announced supplier framework with US Department of War to rapidly increase production of critical defense technologies, including $500M multiyear investment to upgrade production capacity. Notable Decliners: * -31.0% MAZE (Maze Therapeutics, Inc.): MZE829 delivered clinically significant 36% uACR reduction across a broad population of diabetic & non-diabetic AMKD patients; however some cooler takeaways on small sample size and less consistent results across patients; there was one early treatment discontinuation. * -7.5% ONON (On Holding): Announced that Martin Hoffmann has chosen to step down from his position as CEO and CFO, effective 1-May; co-founders David Allemann and Caspar Coppetti will serve as Co-CEOs, while Frank Sluis will join as new CFO. * -5.4% MU (Micron Technology): Memory names lower after GOOGL introduced TurboQuant compression algorithm; said it optimally addresses challenge of memory overhead in vector quantization, achieving a high reduction in model size with zero accuracy loss. * -1.4% WGO (Winnebago Industries): FQ2 earnings and revenue better; noted seasonal factors and weather tempered retail activity; Towables saw lower volumes and mix shift to lower-price models; reaffirmed FY guidance (which bookends the Street). * -1.4% JHG (Janus Henderson Group): Victory Capital (VCTR) announced withdrawal of proposal to acquire company. Top Stories (last modified 07:46 ET on 25-Mar-26) Morning Headlines: * Iran conflict: * US offers Iran 15-point plan to end conflict, includes one-month ceasefire, addresses nuclear and missile programs (NY Times<https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/24/world/middleeast/us-iran-peace-plan.html>, Bloomberg <https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-25/us-drafts-15-point-plan-to-end-iran-war-as-trump-pushes-talks> ) * Tehran denies peace talks progress, asserts US is negotiating only with itself (Reuters<https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/iran-military-spokesperson-says-us-is-negotiating-with-itself-state-media-2026-03-25>, Washington Post <https://www.washingtonpost.com/national-security/2026/03/24/us-iran-peace-talks-pakistan-turkey-egypt> ) * Iranian officials distrust Trump, view peace talks as another trick (Axios<https://www.axios.com/2026/03/25/iran-war-trump-peace-talks-tehran-suspicious>); prefer engaging with VP Vance and Israel launch fresh airstrikes; Iran continues attacks on Gulf states after US peace proposal (Reuters <https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/israel-strikes-tehran-trump-says-us-negotiating-end-war-2026-03-25> , Bloomberg <https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-25/us-awaits-response-to-peace-plan-as-iran-keeps-up-attacks> ) * Trump claims Iran offered "present" relating to oil and gas, optimistic about negotiations (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-24/iran-israel-trade-strikes-amid-us-claims-of-peace-talks>) * US to deploy thousands of paratroopers from 82nd Airborne to Middle East (Washington Post<https://www.washingtonpost.com/national-security/2026/03/24/us-orders-82nd-airborne-iran-paratroopers-kharg/?utm_source=alert&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=wp_news_alert_revere&location=alert>, attacked (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-24/gulf-states-weigh-military-options-to-counter-iran-s-escalation>) * China Foreign Minister urged Iranian counterpart to negotiate end to war as soon as possible (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-24/china-s-wang-yi-pushes-iran-to-talk-with-us-about-ending-war>) * Energy: * Iran says foreign ships not involved in war allowed to transit Strait of Hormuz (FT<https://www.ft.com/content/71c3295a-531a-4fa8-86bc-964b5bb821e2>, NY Times <https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/24/world/middleeast/iran-strait-of-hormuz-ships.html> ) * For Western powers, failed efforts to secure Red Sea shipping signal greater challenges for the Strait of Hormuz (Reuters<https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/western-powers-were-unable-secure-shipping-red-sea-hormuz-will-be-harder-2026-03-25>) * UK proposes hosting an international summit to create a plan for reopening the Strait of Hormuz (Guardian<https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/mar/24/uk-offers-to-host-international-security-summit-on-reopening-strait-of-hormuz>) * Record high Middle East crude prices could cascade to US and elsewhere if peace talks do not lead to resolution soon (link<https://www.msn.com/en-us/money/markets/the-oil-supply-crunch-is-spreading-from-the-gulf-to-the-rest-of-the-world/ar-AA1Zl712>) * High oil and gas prices may persist beyond Trump's Iran conflict, despite promises of quick relief (NY Times <https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/24/us/politics/oil-gas-prices-iran.html> ) * Trump administration preparing to issue waiver expanding availability of higher ethanol E15 gasoline (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-24/trump-officials-ready-e15-waiver-for-summer-gasoline-sales>) * Oil executives say scale of Middle East supply disruption biggest they've seen (Reuters <https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/ceraweek-global-energy-crisis-deepens-efforts-plug-supply-gap-fall-short-2026-03-24> , consider Covid-style measures like remote work to manage fuel shortages (Reuters<https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/asia-looks-covid-era-playbook-tackle-fuel-crisis-2026-03-25>) * Oil and gas firms, including Equinor and a Shell-backed group, urge EU to lift Arctic drilling ban (FT <https://www.ft.com/content/1bb72cf7-5fc6-4ee9-ae76-73f7937407f1?syn-25a6b1a6=1> ) * Corporate: * SpaceX aims <https://www.axios.com/2026/03/24/microsoft-nvidia-ai-nuclear-energy-plants> ) * Apple testing standalone Siri app and give it AI chatbot-like experience (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-24/ios-27-features-apple-ai-reboot-with-siri-app-new-interface-ask-siri-button>) * Jury in New Mexico says Meta must pay $375M for endangering children online (Washington Post<https://www.washingtonpost.com/technology/2026/03/24/meta-jury-harm-children>) * Meta grants stock options to key leaders to retain talent amid intensifying AI pressure (CNBC<https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/24/meta-offers-stock-awards-options-for-executives-aggressive-timing.html>, link <https://www.msn.com/en-us/money/companies/meta-targets-9-trillion-valuation-with-new-executive-incentive-program/ar-AA1Zlnyh> ) * Chinese authorities restrict two Manus co-founders from leaving, increasing scrutiny on Meta's $2B acquisition (FT <https://www.ft.com/content/d9123d9d-c807-41d6-8a17-80ff1111834a?syn-25a6b1a6=1> ) * Arm Holdings to sell its own chips, aiming for $15B in annual revenue within five years; signs up Meta as first customer (Bloomberg <https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-24/arm-to-sell-its-own-chips-for-first-time-in-bid-for-ai-revenue> ) * SK Hynix aiming to list in US this year, seeks to raise $10B (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-24/sk-hynix-to-float-us-shares-this-year-to-feed-ai-memory-surge>, CNBC <https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/25/sk-hynix-confidential-us-listing-adr-ai-memory.html> ) * Federal judge finds Pentagon's treatment of Anthropic "troubling," as court urged to pause supply chain risk designation (Axios<https://www.axios.com/2026/03/24/judge-pentagon-anthropic-troubling>, FT<https://www.ft.com/content/68f6a495-72a5-4dad-9696-8f7a893e3569>) * Anduril and Palantir working to develop software for Golden Dome missile shield program (Reuters<https://ca.finance.yahoo.com/news/anduril-palantir-developing-golden-dome-233842760.html>) * Merck nearing $6B deal to acquire Terns Pharma, boosting pipeline amid Keytruda patent expiration (FT <https://www.ft.com/content/3f695210-8cc0-4736-837a-7961d80588d9?syn-25a6b1a6=1> ) * Blackstone plans to invest $15B in Japan, targeting data centers, logistics facilities and hotels (Nikkei<https://asia.nikkei.com/business/markets/property/blackstone-to-invest-15bn-in-japan-over-three-years>) * Central banks: * Prosecutor concedes no evidence of misconduct by Fed chair Powell, undermining Trump's "criminality" claims on renovations (Washington Post <https://www.washingtonpost.com/national-security/2026/03/24/powell-fed-chair-subpoena-prosecutor> ) * Fed Governor Barr wants evidence inflation falling sustainably before considering further rate cuts (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-24/fed-s-barr-says-rates-may-need-to-hold-steady-for-some-time>) * Chicago Fed's Goolsbee says more progress on inflation needed for rate cuts to be realistic (Reuters<https://www.reuters.com/markets/us/feds-goolsbee-says-inflation-progress-needed-cut-rates-this-year-pbs-reports-2026-03-24>) * ECB President Lagarde states central bank may raise interest rates in April if Iran war inflation escalates (FT <https://www.ft.com/content/2edcaaa4-d8ea-430f-8db4-936fee2bc3e3?syn-25a6b1a6=1> ) * BOJ January meeting minutes showed board members biding time on next rate hike (Reuters<https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/boj-board-members-saw-need-more-rate-hikes-january-minutes-show-2026-03-25>) * RBNZ chief economist Conway says assessment of second-round inflation effects will determine rate hikes (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-25/rbnz-s-conway-sees-economic-slack-shaping-response-to-oil-shock>) * Markets: * Retail investors sold $20.6M in stocks on Monday, marking first net selling since Nov 2023 (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-24/retail-crowd-flips-to-selling-stocks-for-first-time-since-2023>) * Strategists say spike in loan defaults may force out stress from the $3T private credit sector, enabling a "healthy reset" (CNBC <https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/25/private-credit-defaults-loan-quality-debt-risk-systemic-ai-disruption.html> ) * Investors snap up over $18B debt tied to Electronic Arts' $55B acquisition, reflecting strong risk appetite (FT <https://www.ft.com/content/e27a4caf-9b70-4a3f-a2ca-2d555b7f0a0a?syn-25a6b1a6=1> ) * Companies speeding up debt raising plans amid market volatility and political uncertainty (FT <https://www.ft.com/content/747a6191-0320-4c21-a80c-cb9627c45e07?syn-25a6b1a6=1> ) * Treasury yields have logged biggest monthly rise since Oct-2024 amid Middle East turmoil (FT<https://www.ft.com/content/0976a312-e578-4754-99bb-ecdf46a8e8c0>) * Tumbling gold prices challenge its "haven" status; analysts expect a rebound despite ongoing Iran war impacts (FT <https://www.ft.com/content/dbb810ae-0886-40fa-9f2c-e4676eec299c?syn-25a6b1a6=1> ) * China state media talks up token usage, driving rally in China AI stocks (Bloomberg<https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-25/china-s-ai-stocks-gain-as-state-media-tout-rising-token-usage>) * Economy: * China's market regulator addresses unfair competition, urges end to price wars in industry (Reuters<https://www.reuters.com/world/china/chinese-food-delivery-giants-surge-regulator-state-media-call-end-price-war-2026-03-25>, Bloomberg <https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-25/meituan-alibaba-shares-jump-as-beijing-vows-to-end-price-wars> ) * UK inflation steady at 3% in February, ahead of expected energy shock from Middle East conflict (FT<https://www.ft.com/content/88372f6d-4a02-4c13-8814-0a0384b8c06b>, CNBC <https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/25/uk-inflation-february-2026-iran-war-energy-prices-cpi.html> ) * German business morale declines in March due to the Iran war, with Ifo index dropping to 86.4 (Reuters <https://www.reuters.com/business/german-business-sentiment-fell-less-than-expected-march-ifo-finds-2026-03-25> ) * [...truncated to 2000 words]
OVI Scanner Context
MARKET CONTEXT (recent headlines): - [cnbc_markets] Global forecasting group sees U.S. inflation at 4.2% this year, much higher than Fed estimate - [marketwatch] Global oil prices surpass $100 a barrel as Iran war nears one-month mark - [cnbc_markets] Recession odds climb on Wall Street as economy shows cracks beneath the surface - [cnbc_markets] European Central Bank holds rates steady, warns outlook is 'significantly more uncertain' - [ft_markets] US inflation will surge to 4.2% on energy shock, warns OECD - [ft_markets] The number oil traders would love to know - [ft_markets] Oil rises above $107 on doubts over Iran peace talks - [ft_markets] Bessent praises BoE as model for tighter oversight of Fed

ATM IV = at-the-money implied vol  |  IV PCTILE = where today's IV ranks vs ≈90 trading days (green=cheap, red=expensive)  |  Spread = ATM IV − RV 20d  |  Rich: IV/RV >1.2×  |  Skew 90/110: P(90%) − C(110%)  |  SKEW PCTILE: vs ≈90d history  |  PUT/CALL V%: daily volume rank vs ≈90d history  |  vs SECTOR: ticker ATM IV / sector ETF ATM IV (±10% threshold)  |  Divergence: cross-signal flags (VOL HIGH/SKEW FLAT, CHEAP VOL/RICH SKEW, etc.)
